mnemonics: A Half Adder is Half-Baked - it can't carry its own carry-in.
K-Maps: Group Ones, Not Zeros, to Minimize the Drama.
keyPoints: Combinational logic outputs depend ONLY on the current inputs (no memory).
A multiplexer (MUX) acts like a multi-input selector switch.
A decoder activates one output line for each unique input combination.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to account for 'don't care' conditions in K-maps, leading to non-minimized circuits.
Incorrectly grouping cells in a K-map (groups must be powers of two and rectangular).
